topic: Pieces for Developers Review 2026: LTM Memory + MCP Integration

Covers pricing, features, pros and cons in structured review format
Targets readers comparing AI productivity tools

stats.fact: Pieces LTM stores workflow context for 9 months on the free plan, enabling time-based queries like 'What was I working on 3 months ago?'
Pieces Pro plan costs $18.99/month ($14.17/month billed annually), unlocking cloud LLMs including GPT-5, Claude Opus, and Gemini 2.5
The LTM-2.7 engine continuously captures workflow context: code copied, screens viewed, and audio heard — all stored locally on-device via PiecesOS
Pieces MCP Server integrates with Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Goose, Claude Cowork, and OpenClaw as of 2026
The MCP ecosystem grew to 50+ official servers and 150+ community implementations by March 2026
Pieces for Developers earns a 3.9/5 rating on major review platforms as of 2026
Mem0, a competing AI memory tool, is backed by Y Combinator and used by 50,000+ developers, with plans from free to $249/month for graph memory
Pieces MCP's ask_pieces_ltm tool lets AI agents query yesterday's workflow context to auto-generate standup reports and summary documents

angles: The LTM-first differentiator: Why Pieces bets on persistent memory over inline code completion, and what that means for developer productivity
Privacy-first AI memory: Pieces' on-device architecture vs. cloud-based alternatives like Mem0 — who should care and why
MCP as the force multiplier: How connecting Pieces to Cursor, Claude Cowork, or Goose transforms raw workflow data into automated standups and summaries
Free tier deep dive: What you actually get with Pieces' no-cost plan (9-month memory, local AI, unlimited snippets) and where it hits a wall
Real developer concern: Honest look at CPU usage, update bugs, and collaboration gaps that prevent Pieces from being a universal recommendation
